guest_role: Founder and Managing Partner of SoftTech VC
assertiveness_why: Harry asks well-prepared, specific multi-sentence questions with genuine follow-ups (Fitbit story, Brad Feld relationship, seed round sizing) but never challenges or contests any of Jeff's claims.
question_style: Sequenced, well-researched questions building on prior answers (Fitbit backstory, deal flow prioritization, seed round sizing, preemption), with light playful asides ('Has Brad brought you a present since?') but no interruptions or pushback.
tone: Highly complimentary and fan-like ('true legend of the industry', 'ecstatic to have you'), energetic and warm, includes a sponsor read at open and close.
depth: Good tactical/strategic depth on seed fund mechanics: deal flow volume and referral prioritization, 18-month runway sizing by sector, and preemptive term sheets — reflects real familiarity with venture mechanics though not adversarial.
harry_quotes: How did you convince Brad then?
Has Brad brought you a present since?
Do you play a very dominant role in raising that money?
learnings: Fitbit was nearly universally passed on by VCs at every round despite strong early demand signals, illustrating how consensus can be badly wrong on hardware.
SoftTech prioritizes warm introductions from trusted portfolio founders over cold inbound, since deal volume (3,000+/year) makes referral strength the main filter.
Seed rounds are deliberately sized for about 18 months of runway (more for hardware) so founders get room to iterate through a failed plan A before hitting a series A wall.
About a third of SoftTech's portfolio companies get preemptive term sheets rather than running a formal fundraising process, which VCs now use to avoid bidding wars rather than to get a cheap price.
Seed investors deliberately map which of a handful of series A firms specialize in a company's specific sector (hardware, marketplaces, etc.) and cultivate those relationships months in advance.
repeat_guest_callback: I recently had Brad Feld on the show and obviously he mentioned you alongside him in the Fitbit IPO.
notable: Features a detailed, first-hand account of nearly every VC repeatedly passing on Fitbit despite its eventual multi-billion dollar IPO.
